rulings.Case Title: PROFESSIONAL COLLECTION CONSULTANTS VS. KIM M TAM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 5, 2015, Line 9, PLAINTIFF PROFESSIONAL COLLECTION CONSULTANTS' HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. Denied without prejudice. No property is exempt from enforcement unless specifically made so by law; and courts may not increase statutory exemptions. (Estate of Brown (1899) 123 Cal. 399, 401.) CCP sec. 706.051, the statute that exempts property necessary for support, only applies to wage garnishment. Because this claim involves a bank levy, CCP sec. 706.051 does not apply. Thus, Defendant has not established a statutory exemption. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DARRYL LEUNG VS. TONY HSAIO TANG TU, DOING BUSINESS AS H.T. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Plaintiff Leungs Motion For Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt And For Monetary Sanctions For Failure To Submit To Deposition Pursuant To Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Monday, January 5, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT TONY HSAIO TANG TU's Joinder To Plaintiff Leung's Motion For Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt And For Monetary Sanctions For Failure To Submit To Deposition Pursuant To Subpoena. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted in part and denied in part. Jin Quing Li a.k.a. James Li shall appear at his deposition within ten (10) court days on the date, time and place designated by Plaintiff. No sanctions are awarded against Mr. Li as this would likely violate bankruptcy law. No sanctions against Mr. Kass. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tomcohen@ionix.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TERRENCE BARNUM VS. PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Furthere Response To Form Interrogatories-Employment Law (Set One), And Req. For Production Of Documents (Set One)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 5, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF TERRENCE BARNUM'S Motion To Compel Further Response To Form Interrogatories-Employment Law (Set One), And Request For Production Of Documents (Set One). Granted in part and denied in part. As to Special Interrogatory No. 1, the motion is granted. Defendant shall respond to Plaintiff's proposed limited interrogatory: "As to the documents produced or designated as privileged in response to the request for production of documents, where were the documents kept in the normal course of business?" As to the Draft Memorandum, the motion is denied. The document is protected by attorney-client privilege and Defendant has not waived that privilege. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: OUTFITTER VENTURES, LLC VS. WIRELESS COMMUNICATION INTERACTIVE INCORPORATED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 5, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT JAY AJALTOUNI'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Denied. CCP sec. 473(b) specifies that an application for relief under the statute must be made "no more than six months after entry of judgment." "The six month time limit for granting relief under section 473 is jurisdictional and relief cannot be granted under section 473 if the application for such relief is instituted more than six months after the entry of the judgment¿" (Aldrich v. San Fernando Valley Lumber Co. (1985) 170 Cal.App.3d 725, 735, fn. 3.) Default judgment was entered on April 14, 2014. Defendant's motion was not filed until October 15, 2014, beyond the six month period. Based on the plain language of CCP sec. 473(b), the motion must be denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 5, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA AND UTAH INSURANCE'S Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend because the Second Amended Complaint fails to state a cause of action for declaratory relief and the defect cannot be cured by further amendment. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties should be prepared to address whether or not the referee can or should be the person to determine the discovery issues. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BINH TRAN ET AL VS. LAMBDA PHI EPSILON NATIONAL FRATERNITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order To Produce Autopsy Photographs Of Plaintiffs' Decendant Peter Tran
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Monday, January 5, 2015, Line 10, PLAINTIFFs BINH TRAN and XUAN NGUYEN's Motion For An Order To Produce Autopsy Photographs Of Plaintiffs' Decendant Peter Tran. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted without prejudice to a defense motion to exclude the photographs at trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tomcohen@ionix.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)
